name: Motril and Salobreña Vega Loop
name_original: Castillo de Salobreña y playas de Motril  — vuelta desde Motril
description: This easy 16.6-mile road cycling route from Motril to Salobreña Castle offers coastal views and tropical fruit plantations.
region_intro: Road cycling around Motril offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal routes with mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by its unique orography, allowing cyclists to ascend from sea level to elevations over 2,000 meters. The imposing Sierra Nevada mountains provide a dramatic backdrop to many routes, while the Mediterranean coastline features coves and beaches. Fertile agricultural plains with tropical fruit plantations also add a distinct scenic element to the rides. Best road cycling routes around Motril The most popular road cycling route is Haza del Lino Loop via Rubite and Polopos, a 56.4 miles (90.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Motril and Salobreña Vega Loop, an easy 16.6 miles (26.8 km) path. This route offers coastal views and passes through tropical fruit plantations with minimal elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Jete Open Door to the Valley – View of Salobreña loop from Motril, a 34.8 miles (56.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Río Verde Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Motril is defined by its Mediterranean coastline, tropical fruit plantations, and the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Motril are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 4,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Motril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Motril?
A: Motril offers a wide array of road cycling routes, with over 40 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from gentle coastal rides to demanding mountain climbs.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Motril?
A: Road cycling around Motril provides incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll experience everything from the Mediterranean coastline with its charming coves and expansive beaches to the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra Nevada mountains. Many routes also pass through fertile agricultural plains dotted with lush tropical fruit plantations, such as mangoes and avocados. You might also encounter impressive structures like the Rules Dam and explore scenic areas like the Río Verde Valley.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Motril?
A: Yes, Motril offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981456520/motril-and-salobrena-vega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motril and Salobreña Vega Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 16.6-mile (26.8 km) path. This route provides coastal views and passes through tropical fruit plantations with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling climbs around Motril?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Motril offers demanding routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981389822/haza-del-lino-loop-via-rubite-and-polopos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza del Lino Loop via Rubite and Polopos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 56.4-mile (90.8 km) trail featuring substantial climbs and panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38205972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Rules Dam – Nazari Bridge of Tablate loop from Playa de Poniente&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 90 km with significant elevation.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available in Motril?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Motril are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular loop routes include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981389822/haza-del-lino-loop-via-rubite-and-polopos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza del Lino Loop via Rubite and Polopos&lt;/a&gt;, the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981456520/motril-and-salobrena-vega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motril and Salobreña Vega Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31971888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jete Open Door to the Valley – View of Salobreña loop from Motril&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see near the cycling routes in Motril?
A: Beyond the stunning coastal and mountain views, you can explore several natural attractions near Motril's cycling routes. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4692443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca Suárez&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful lake, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2648845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Playa de la Joya&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4968963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalfeo River Spring in Salobreña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3403842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ítrabo Spring&lt;/a&gt; also offer picturesque natural stops.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Motril?
A: The road cycling routes in Motril are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's unique blend of coastal beauty and mountainous challenges, the stunning panoramic views, and the variety of terrain suitable for all levels.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of the Sierra Nevada mountains?
A: Absolutely. The imposing Sierra Nevada mountains provide a dramatic backdrop to many cycling routes around Motril. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981389822/haza-del-lino-loop-via-rubite-and-polopos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza del Lino Loop via Rubite and Polopos&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs that reward you with panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Which routes pass through tropical fruit plantations?
A: Several routes in Motril offer the unique experience of cycling through lush tropical fruit plantations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981456520/motril-and-salobrena-vega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motril and Salobreña Vega Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that specifically passes through these fertile agricultural plains, showcasing mango, avocado, and custard apple trees.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Motril?
A: Motril benefits from a mild subtropical climate with over 320 sunny days a year, making it suitable for cycling for most of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures, while winter is also mild. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes may pass near historical sites. For instance, the area around Motril has a rich history, including sugar cane production. You might also find historical points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5549933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Toro Mines&lt;/a&gt;, which are historical sites that can be explored.
tips: The castle of Salobreña is a very characteristic construction of this town that can be seen from many sides of the area, not only because of its height but also because of the contrast with the white houses of the town. The Arab castle is the result of the contribution of Muslim and Christian architecture since the 10th century.
The Kings of Belgium, Baudouin and Fabiola, built their dream retreat on this beach, and they enjoyed it very discreetly during the summers until King Baudouin died here in 1993.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is a return trip from Motril, you can find parking in both Salobreña and Motril. For Salobreña, parking near the historic center or closer to the beaches would be suitable. In Motril, look for parking near the beaches or the town center to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is largely considered easy and suitable for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. The terrain is predominantly flat along the coastline with only gentle undulations, making it accessible for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features paved surfaces, ideal for road bikes. You'll cycle along coastal roads, through fertile plains, and river valleys. The terrain is generally flat, with some gentle rises as you move between towns and along beach promenades.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of coastal vistas and agricultural scenery. You'll pass by the iconic Salobreña Castle, enjoy the Mediterranean coastline near Playa de la Cagadilla, and cycle through areas with tropical fruit plantations like custard apple, mango, and avocado.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa Tropical region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions year-round. However, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and less crowded conditions than the peak summer months.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several local paths. You'll find sections that follow the Sendero Fluvial del Rio Guadalfeo, Senda Litoral, Camino Cazorla, and Camino de los Pescadores, among others.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on paved roads and passes through urban and agricultural areas, it's generally not ideal for dogs to accompany cyclists on a racebike. If you plan to stop at beaches, be aware of local regulations regarding dogs on sand.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay fees to cycle this public road and coastal route between Salobreña and Motril.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Salobreña and Motril, as well as the coastal areas between them, offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and chiringuitos (beach bars) where you can stop for refreshments, especially near the beaches and town centers.

faqs.question: What are the unique natural features I'll encounter?
faqs.answer: A unique aspect of this route is cycling through areas rich with subtropical fruit farms, characteristic of the Costa Tropical. You'll also experience diverse coastal vistas and may follow sections along the River Guadalfeo, adding a riparian element to the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the coastal road busy with traffic?
faqs.answer: The N340 coastal road, which connects many towns along the Costa Tropical, is now quieter due to newer motorways. This provides a more pleasant and scenic cycling experience away from heavy traffic for much of the route.
